raw_persona.narrative:
name: Maximo Esteban Irizarry
age: 67
sex: Male
marital_status: married_present
education_level: high_school
occupation: software_developer
hobbies_and_interests: Hiking, Photography, Acrylic painting, Acoustic guitar, Cooking Puerto Rican cuisine, Reading magical‑realist literature, Journaling, Volunteer coding workshops, Small art‑tech meet‑ups
skills_and_expertise: Python, PHP, JavaScript, React, HTML5/CSS3, Node.js, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Git, Docker, GitHub Actions, Agile/Scrum, UI/UX design, Accessibility (WCAG), Data visualisation, Mentoring, Community teaching
complementary_info: Maximo Esteban Irizarry was born and raised in Orocovis and is deeply shaped by jíbaro culture, with family roots in small coffee and tropical fruit plots and a life attuned to seasonal rhythms. Spanish is his first language, and he is conversational in English. Catholic traditions remain part of family gatherings, especially during fiestas patronales, with bomba and plena music, dance, and communal meals. He takes early-morning hikes on Orocovis mountain trails, often with the local Orocovis Hiking Club, and accommodates arthritis with low-impact strength routines; he also sometimes plays casual basketball at the community gym and follows the Vaqueros baseball team. His photography centers on sunrise vistas and black-and-white prints, and his painting often depicts rural landscapes and jíbaro scenes. He plays boleros and nueva canción on acoustic guitar, occasionally joining small jam sessions at the community centre, and he attends local gallery openings. He reads authors such as Laura Esquivel and Quiñones, keeps a reflective journal to manage anxiety and emotional reactivity, and records recipes in a well-worn scrapbook while cooking dishes like arroz con gandules, pasteles, and lechón with occasional modern variations. After more than forty years in software work, he is moving toward part-time community-centered consultancy for small Orocovis businesses, emphasizing low-bandwidth, low-maintenance, culturally resonant websites, while also pursuing a long-term plan to build a digital archive of Puerto Rican folk stories, music, and visual art and eventually a modest local tech hub for small-group mentoring. His travel pattern favors quiet, culturally rich places such as Cartagena, Oaxaca, and the Azores, usually planned around modest savings, off-season timing, and sometimes staying with family in nearby coastal villages.

structured_persona.name: Maximo Esteban Irizarry
structured_persona.persona_summary: Maximo Irizarry, a 67‑year‑old tech mentor with a quiet demeanor, merges mountain hikes, vintage photography, and soulful guitar strums into a life that balances meticulous coding with spontaneous artistic bursts, all while wrestling with bouts of anxiety that sometimes derail their disciplined routines.
structured_persona.professional_persona: Maximo Irizarry, a seasoned full‑stack software developer with over forty years of experience, blends Python, PHP, and React expertise with a deep love of art to craft low‑bandwidth, accessible web solutions for small Orocovis businesses, while mentoring junior coders and guiding a part‑time consultancy that envisions a digital archive of folk stories; they balance meticulous Agile practices with a flexible, community‑first mindset.
structured_persona.sports_persona: Maximo Irizarry, an early‑morning hiker who frequents the Orocovis mountain trails with the local Orocovis Hiking Club, also enjoys occasional casual basketball games with friends at the community gym and cheers for the regional Vaqueros baseball team, balancing solitary cardio with low‑impact strength routines that accommodate their arthritis.
structured_persona.arts_persona: Maximo Irizarry, an avid black‑and‑white photographer and acrylic painter of rural landscapes, finds inspiration in boleros by Silvio Rodríguez and the lyrical verses of magical‑realist authors like Laura Esquivel, while they occasionally join intimate acoustic jam sessions at the community centre and attend local gallery openings featuring artists such as José Campeche.
structured_persona.travel_persona: Maximo Irizarry, a deliberate traveler who prefers tranquil, culturally‑rich destinations, has explored the historic streets of Cartagena, the artisanal markets of Oaxaca, and the volcanic scenery of the Azores, while they plan each trip around modest savings, opting for off‑season travel and staying with family in nearby coastal villages when budgets tighten.
structured_persona.culinary_persona: Maximo Irizarry, a proficient home chef, delights in preparing traditional dishes like arroz con gandules, pasteles, and lechón de cerdo, often adding modern twists such as quinoa‑infused rice or citrus‑marinated pork, and they relish seasoning with sofrito, cilantro, and adobo while documenting recipes in a well‑worn scrapbook.
structured_persona.cultural_background: Born and raised in Orocovis, the mountainous heart of Puerto Rico, Maximo’s identity is steeped in jíbaro culture—a blend of Spanish colonial, African, and indigenous Taino influences. His family has long cultivated coffee and tropical fruits on small plots, fostering a deep respect for the land and seasonal rhythms. Spanish is his first language, and he is conversational in English, a skill sharpened by his tech career. Catholic traditions shape family gatherings, especially during fiestas patronales, where bomba and plena music, traditional dance, and communal meals reinforce communal bonds. Maximo participates in local festivals that celebrate folk art, storytelling, and the island’s natural beauty, preserving these customs through his digital projects. The close‑knit community of Villalba Municipio and the broader Puerto Rican diaspora inform his values of humility, cooperation, and cultural pride.
structured_persona.skills_and_expertise: Maximo Esteban Irizarry is a seasoned software developer with more than four decades of practical experience. His core technical expertise lies in full‑stack web development, where he builds back‑end services with Python and PHP and front‑end interfaces using JavaScript, React, and HTML5/CSS3. He is proficient with relational databases such as MySQL and PostgreSQL and applies advanced query optimisation to support low‑bandwidth rural connections. Maximo follows modern version‑control practices with Git and implements continuous integration pipelines using Docker and GitHub Actions. His familiarity with Agile and Scrum methods reflects his balanced approach to organization and flexibility. A lifelong curiosity about art and unconventional ideas has led him to integrate UI/UX design principles, accessibility standards (WCAG), and interactive data visualisations into his projects, creating empathetic digital experiences. He also mentors junior developers and leads community coding workshops, emphasising clear documentation and collaborative problem solving.
structured_persona.hobbies_and_interests: Maximo finds solace in solitary and small‑group activities that blend his love of nature, art, and culture. He enjoys early‑morning hikes along the Orocovis mountain trails, often capturing sunrise vistas with his DSLR camera and later creating black‑and‑white prints. Acrylic painting of rural landscapes and traditional jíbaro scenes allows him to express his artistic curiosity. He plays acoustic guitar, favouring boleros and nueva canción, and occasionally joins intimate jam sessions at the local community centre. Cooking classic Puerto Rican dishes such as arroz con gandules, pasteles, and lechón de cerdo connects him to family traditions, and he experiments with modern twists. He reads magical‑realist literature by authors like Esquivel and Quiñones, and he maintains a reflective journal to manage his emotional reactivity. Volunteering to teach basic coding to youths and organizing small art‑tech meet‑ups satisfy his cooperative nature while keeping his social circles manageable.
structured_persona.career_goals_and_ambitions: Approaching retirement, Maximo aims to shift his professional focus toward mentorship and community impact while preserving his own well‑being. He plans to formalise a part‑time consultancy that assists small local businesses in Orocovis to modernise their online presence, ensuring solutions are low‑maintenance and culturally resonant. A central ambition is to develop a digital platform that archives Puerto Rican folk stories, music, and visual art, allowing younger generations to explore their heritage through interactive media. He also hopes to contribute to open‑source projects that improve accessibility for low‑bandwidth environments. By establishing a modest tech hub in his hometown, Maximo wishes to guide aspiring developers, fostering collaborative learning in small, supportive groups that align with his preference for intimacy and harmony. Financial stability for his family and a sustainable work rhythm that mitigates anxiety are key drivers of his career roadmap.

rules.rule_text: When a booked trip is disrupted, he prefers replacing failed stops with alternatives in the same cultural or nature theme rather than swapping the whole trip.

rules.counterfactual_default: Replace the full itinerary or choose any convenient substitute regardless of theme.

rules.rule_text: For long-distance travel, he prefers ground transport over flights when that option fits the trip.
rules.canonical_answer: When I can, I prefer going by ground rather than flying.
rules.counterfactual_default: Look for and book flights first for long-distance travel.
